# Decisions: felt-cost

- 2026-07-12T12:13:31.569Z — chose **Cycle scope = the memo's three vetted plays (cold-resume advisory, rent-meter with cache-read health bands, batch-complete nudge behind SPEC) plus a byte-stability regression test as the residue of the report's cache-anchoring play 1a** over Building cache-anchoring as new work, or adopting report Tier-2 (model-tiered/batch bookkeeping, delta injection) because The 1a property already exists (renderStatus embeds no volatile bytes) — pin it against silent regression rather than build what's already true. Report Stage-1 hardening confirmed already shipped (independent confirmation the engine was built right, not wasted research). User-approved Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:13:40.584Z — chose **Any future cache-cost play must cite token-optimization's rejected approach "leading with prompt caching (doesn't reduce occupancy)" as an informed re-test — legitimate because the scope differs (dollar cost vs token occupancy)** over Treating the recorded rejection as blocking, or ignoring it as out-of-scope because The M4 convention is the M4 convention: informed re-tests that cite the recorded decision are legitimate; silent re-proposal is not. The record governing its own evolution is the product working on itself. User ruling Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:13:48.974Z — chose **"sofar makes zero model API calls" promoted to a named architectural invariant (docs/SPEC.md + CLAUDE.md guard-rails); report Tier-2 (Haiku/Batch bookkeeping, 2a/2b) deferred entirely** over Adopting model-tiered or Batch-API bookkeeping, or leaving the property implicit as an accident of implementation because No API keys, no inference costs, no user content sent anywhere; write-backs are already minimal by construction because they are the agent's own tool-call args (report 2c true by construction). Trading that for the measured ~$3.87/2-day upside would be a terrible exchange. User ruling Jul 12.
- 2026-07-12T12:25:52.765Z — chose **sofar statusline (3.1 SPEC-lite): a read-side CLI subcommand for Claude Code's statusLine slot — reads statusline JSON from stdin, prints ONE plain line "slug done/total · $cost · cache N%[⚠|✓] · ctx N%", omits any segment it cannot compute, appends nothing to the record, always exits 0. Health marker judged only after 10k tokens: &lt;30% warm ⚠ (prefix nondeterminism), ≥50% ✓. Root resolution: --root/cwd, falling back to the JSON's workspace.current_dir then cwd** over Auto-installing the statusLine in sofar init (clobbers user config — README documents the one-liner instead), ui-kernel/ANSI styling (statusline is a contract-byte surface, keep plain), or serving cost via the localhost state server (statusline stdin already carries cost + cache tokens at zero API cost) because The rent-meter must satisfy the zero-model-API invariant: pure stdin→stdout derivation, no keys, no calls. A statusline must never break or mutate — hooks' best-effort philosophy (BD22) applies verbatim. Bands from the Jul-12 research: healthy stable-prefix workloads run 50–80% cache-read; below 30% signals prefix non-determinism — itself a sofar self-diagnostic.
- 2026-07-12T12:31:55.769Z — chose **Batch-complete nudge (4.1 SPEC): a fifth shim, user-prompt-submit.sh → `sofar event user-prompt` (UserPromptSubmit). When the session is registered AND initiative drift since the last write-back ≥5 mechanical events, emit ONE line of additionalContext nudging sofar_end_session while context is warm; else silence. Stateless and re-fires each prompt until write-back (staleness-line precedent). Mid-session repeat write-backs are blessed: fold is already last-wins (ended/summary/next_action overwritten, freshness resets, Stop passes once any session_ended exists)** over Nudging via the Stop hook only (too late — the forced block costs an extra cold turn, $0.65/block measured), a stateful once-per-batch memory (violates read-side derivation), or nudging unregistered sessions (noise in unbound/foreign repos) because UserPromptSubmit stdout at exit 0 becomes additionalContext that lands AFTER the cached prefix (cache-safe, per the Jul-12 research); write-backs that happen in-flow while context is warm make the Stop gate a fallback instead of a tax. Best-effort per BD22: any failure is silence, never a blocked prompt.
